behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is module handoff (manifest parser + dependency graph resolver + checker input), not just single-function logic. No explicit requirement for real external API/OS/subprocess, no AI dependence, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against fixture projects (e.g., npm with lockfile, pip with transitive deps) and assert the vulnerability-check input set contains every direct and transitive dependency/version expected from the resolved graph.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real external API query/response contract for version-range semantics; mocked API can hide parameter-shape/contract mismatches. System-triad trigger: verify real external happy-path response contract.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability DB endpoint (or official staging), submit dependencies where package name matches but version ranges differ, and assert returned CVEs correspond only to affected manifest ranges.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering logic inside one boundary; higher-level checks add no new reality signal for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE fixtures into sort function and assert exact order by severity then CVSS descending (or defined tie-break).

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Needs parser/rewriter + manifest I/O round-trip across modules; unit-only checks can miss rewrite side effects on neighboring entries. No real external infra required.
behaviors.test_description: Run update flow on real manifest fixtures, write output, and diff before/after to assert only the targeted dependency version changed and all other keys/values remain identical.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository side effect and externally visible verification. System-triad trigger: prove external side effect via round-trip readback.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real test repo, then verify via repository API/UI that one PR was created and its diff contains exactly one dependency version change.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic semver rule evaluation in one logic boundary; no multi-module or infra dependence required.
behaviors.test_description: Assert semver comparator marks X.Y.Z -> (X+1).0.0 as breaking and non-major bumps as non-breaking across edge cases (pre-release/build metadata as specified).

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff (semver decision -> PR service suppression -> output flag emission). Rejected System because behavior text requires skip + surfaced scanner output, not proof on a real repository boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run full scanner pipeline with a fixture vulnerability requiring major bump; assert PR client is not invoked and scanner output includes the review-required flag for that dependency.
